projectDescription: A comprehensive 260+ lesson, 20-phase open-source course that takes learners from linear algebra to autonomous agent swarms, producing reusable prompts, skills, agents, and MCP servers along the way.
keyFeatures: 260+ lessons across 20 phases covering the full AI engineering stack
AI-native learning with built-in Claude Code skills (/find-your-level, /check-understanding)
Every lesson ships a reusable artifact: prompts, skills, agents, or MCP servers
Build-from-scratch approach: implement algorithms before using frameworks
Multi-language curriculum: Python, TypeScript, Rust, and Julia
Covers math foundations through autonomous agent swarms (~290 hours)
Open-source with MIT license; community contributions welcome
publicApiSurface: /find-your-level — Claude Code skill: 10-question quiz that maps knowledge to a starting phase
/check-understanding <phase> — Claude Code skill: per-phase quiz with 8 questions and targeted review
outputs/prompts/ — prompt templates for every AI task
outputs/skills/ — SKILL.md files for Claude Code, Cursor, or any AI agent
outputs/agents/ — agent definitions ready to deploy
outputs/mcp-servers/ — MCP servers built during the course
